title: Sultana's Dream By Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain | line by line explanation in Hindi
description:
What if the world you lived in was suddenly flipped — where men hid behind curtains and women commanded science, politics, and peace? That is the radical dare at the heart of Sultana's Dream, written in 1905 by Begum Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain, a text that uses fantasy not as escape but as a surgical critique of patriarchy and gender oppression. Essential for BA English Honours students across DU, SOL, and IGNOU, this feminist classic feels less like a century-old story and more like an argument that never stopped being necessary.
